title: Election of 1860 and secession
description: Test your knowledge on the presidential election of 1860 and the subsequent secession of the South!

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3f2a7c9120214fea4d2b9b7ff54002f4.svg)


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29a456a5887421f65f95bdbfcbcd45cf.svg)

**The pattern depicted in the maps resulted most directly from which of the following factors?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise in sectional tension over the expansion of slavery to western territories
the passage of federal legislation that eliminated the Atlantic Slave Trade
the ratification of an amendment that banned slavery in the South
the creation of tariffs that negatively affected the agricultural industry

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3f2a7c9120214fea4d2b9b7ff54002f4.svg)


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29a456a5887421f65f95bdbfcbcd45cf.svg)

**The major patterns on the maps best support which of the following statements?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the states that seceded from the Union did not give Lincoln electoral votes
the states that supported third-party candidates joined the Union
the states that supported the Republican Party became border states
the states where Douglas won electoral votes seceded from the Union

assessment_items.question_content:
![The artist depicts Uncle Sam (the personification of the United States) on the left arguing with a woman (the personification of the Confederate States). Broken furniture surrounds them. Uncle Sam is holding half of a torn map that has “United” written at the top. The woman is holding the other half of the map, which has “States” written at the top. In the background, a black man tiptoes behind them to escape.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b49290cdd3aa7c57ee2ac4a0ce90358f.png)

>-Source: Brady’s National Photographic Portrait Galleries, “A Family Quarrel,” [Library of Congress,](https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2010647790/) between 1861 and 1870

**The image most closely reflects which of the following developments in the political climate in the United States?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the debate over extending slavery to western territories
the conflict over the construction of the transcontinental railroad
the competition between the United States and Britain for Chinese trade
the war between Mexico and the United States over boundary lines

assessment_items.question_content:
![The artist depicts Uncle Sam (the personification of the United States) on the left arguing with a woman (the personification of the Confederate States). Broken furniture surrounds them. Uncle Sam is holding half of a torn map that has “United” written at the top. The woman is holding the other half of the map, which has “States” written at the top. In the background, a black man tiptoes behind them to escape.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b49290cdd3aa7c57ee2ac4a0ce90358f.png)

>-Source: Brady’s National Photographic Portrait Galleries, “A Family Quarrel,” [Library of Congress,](https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2010647790/) between 1861 and 1870

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the secession of the southern states from the United States
the election of 1860, which gave the Whig candidate the presidency
the Supreme Court decision in *Dred Scott v. Sandford*
the acquisition of territory after victory in the Mexican-American War

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The issue before the country is the extinction of slavery. No man of common sense, who has observed the progress of events, and who is not prepared to surrender the institution, with the safety and independence of the South, can doubt that the time for action has come—now or never. The Southern States are now in the crisis of their fate; and, if we read aright the signs of the times, nothing is needed for our deliverance, but that the ball of revolution be set in motion.”

>-Source: *The Charleston Mercury,* “What Shall the South Carolina Legislature Do?” November 3, 1860

**Which of the following most directly contributed to the sentiments expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Republican Party nominating Lincoln as a presidential candidate
the Democratic Party’s control of both the Senate and the House
the introduction of the Crittenden Compromise to the House
the secession of several southern states to form a confederacy

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The issue before the country is the extinction of slavery. No man of common sense, who has observed the progress of events, and who is not prepared to surrender the institution, with the safety and independence of the South, can doubt that the time for action has come—now or never. The Southern States are now in the crisis of their fate; and, if we read aright the signs of the times, nothing is needed for our deliverance, but that the ball of revolution be set in motion.”

>-Source: *The Charleston Mercury,* “What Shall the South Carolina Legislature Do?” November 3, 1860

**Which of the following developments best represents a logical extension of the ideas expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the formation of the Confederate States of America
the introduction of the Kansas-Nebraska Act
the annexation of Kansas as a new state
the Supreme Court decision in *Dred Scott v. Sandford*

assessment_items.question_content:
![Four men riding donkeys are shown carrying flags that have “Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, and Florida” written on them. They are following a man riding a pig chasing a butterfly who is holding a flag that has “South Carolina” written on it. All five men are shown as heading toward a cliff. Another man riding a donkey and holding a flag with “Georgia” written on it rides downhill.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d20f08766bf2568c7153764781f324d1.png)

>-Source: “The ‘Secession Movement,’ [Library of Congress](http://loc.gov/pictures/resource/pga.05004/),1861

**Which of the following changes to the United States during the nineteenth century most directly contributed to the development depicted in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the election of Lincoln to the presidency in 1860
the abolition of slavery after the Emancipation Proclamation
the end to American participation in the Atlantic Slave Trade
the closing of Southern ports to foreign trade

assessment_items.question_content:
![Four men riding donkeys are shown carrying flags that have “Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, and Florida” written on them. They are following a man riding a pig chasing a butterfly who is holding a flag that has “South Carolina” written on it. All five men are shown as heading toward a cliff. Another man riding a donkey and holding a flag with “Georgia” written on it rides downhill.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d20f08766bf2568c7153764781f324d1.png)

>-Source: “The ‘Secession Movement,’ [Library of Congress](http://loc.gov/pictures/resource/pga.05004/),1861

**The attitudes of the Southern states depicted in the political cartoon contributed to which of the following developments in the mid-nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the start of a war between the Union and the Confederate states
the acquisition of Puerto Rico and Hawaii as American territories
the expansion of the United States’s borders to the Pacific Ocean
the passage of legislation to support the creation of the Transcontinental Railroad

assessment_items.question_content:
![Four men riding donkeys are shown carrying flags that have “Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, and Florida” written on them. They are following a man riding a pig chasing a butterfly who is holding a flag that has “South Carolina” written on it. All five men are shown as heading toward a cliff. Another man riding a donkey and holding a flag with “Georgia” written on it rides downhill.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d20f08766bf2568c7153764781f324d1.png)

>-Source: “The ‘Secession Movement,’ [Library of Congress](http://loc.gov/pictures/resource/pga.05004/),1861

**The image most directly reflects which of the following developments during the mid-nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the formal withdrawal of several southern states from the Union
the growing zeal for westward expansion into newly-acquired territory
the increasing desire for new trading partners in Asia and Europe
the introduction of several new western states after the Mexican-American War

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Yesterday, November the 7th, will long be a memorable day in Charleston. The tea has been thrown overboard; the revolution of 1860 has been initiated. Intense though quiet excitement prevails throughout the community. The Government officials, as our columns will show, have resigned. . . . The Federal officers who have resigned their places are expected to address the meeting to assemble as soon as the Legislature shall have acted. Charleston is not behind the State, and will play her part in the grand drama now before us, as becomes her intelligence, her stake and her civilization. On every lip is the stern cry ‘vive la liberta!’”

>-Source: *The Charleston Mercury,* November 8, 1860


**The excerpt provided is best understood in the context of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the election of Abraham Lincoln
the start of the Civil War
the secession of Missouri
the Dred Scott decision

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Yesterday, November the 7th, will long be a memorable day in Charleston. The tea has been thrown overboard; the revolution of 1860 has been initiated. Intense though quiet excitement prevails throughout the community. The Government officials, as our columns will show, have resigned. . . . The Federal officers who have resigned their places are expected to address the meeting to assemble as soon as the Legislature shall have acted. Charleston is not behind the State, and will play her part in the grand drama now before us, as becomes her intelligence, her stake and her civilization. On every lip is the stern cry ‘vive la liberta!’”

>-Source: *The Charleston Mercury,* November 8, 1860


**Which of the following groups would have been most likely to support the editor’s views expressed in this excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: southern Democrats
northern Republicans
Federalists
Abolitionists

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Yesterday, November the 7th, will long be a memorable day in Charleston. The tea has been thrown overboard; the revolution of 1860 has been initiated. Intense though quiet excitement prevails throughout the community. The Government officials, as our columns will show, have resigned. . . . The Federal officers who have resigned their places are expected to address the meeting to assemble as soon as the Legislature shall have acted. Charleston is not behind the State, and will play her part in the grand drama now before us, as becomes her intelligence, her stake and her civilization. On every lip is the stern cry ‘vive la liberta!’”

>-Source: *The Charleston Mercury,* November 8, 1860


**Which of the following developments best represents a logical extension of the ideas expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the secession of several southern states
the passage of the Crittenden Compromise
the demise of the Whig Party in the South
the introduction of the Homestead Act
